recipes.name: CineChrome
recipes.film: Author original — teal-orange cinema look with deep blues and high dynamic range
recipes.tip: Sits between Cine1 and S-Log — strong in low light with high dynamic range, so it holds up at night too.

recipes.name: Pro Color
recipes.film: Author original — red-cyan harmony leaning yellow, super soft tones, high saturation
recipes.tip: The author pairs it with a diffusion filter for its artistic, soft look.

recipes.name: CineStill 50D
recipes.film: Repackaged Kodak Vision3 50D cine film — fine grain, pastel daylight colors, halation
recipes.tip: A daylight-balanced recipe that shines on sunny days; add a diffusion filter for extra punch.

recipes.name: CineStill BwXX (Cinestill X)
recipes.film: CineStill BwXX (Kodak Double-X) — classic cinema B&W with deep blacks and drama
recipes.tip: Keeps its deep tonal range even when overexposed; formerly named 'Cinestill X', now 'Cinestill X - BwXX'.

recipes.name: Kodak T-Max 100
recipes.film: Kodak's fine-grain B&W, prized for sharpness and smooth, modern tonality

recipes.name: Kodak Tri-X 400
recipes.film: Legendary Kodak B&W press film — at box speed a soft, low-contrast 'muddy' look
recipes.tip: Expose carefully — underexposed it turns muddy; best for lo-fi portraits and casual events.

recipes.name: Kodak Tri-X 1600
recipes.film: Tri-X pushed to 1600 — the gritty, contrasty street look most people associate with it
recipes.tip: Raise or lower ISO to dial grain to taste, and turn off noise reduction to keep the gritty texture.

recipes.name: X-Tarr
recipes.film: Author original — the deepest, crunchiest B&W: ultra-deep blacks, bold contrast
recipes.tip: Embrace the crushed blacks — it suits shots where the subject is the story; skip it if you want soft, airy tones.

recipes.name: Chroma Fade
recipes.film: Author original — heavily compressed faded B&W, like dusty prints found in an attic
recipes.tip: Highlights max out around 75% with faded shadows — lean into it for soft, aged, melancholic scenes.

recipes.name: Agfa CT Precisa 100
recipes.film: Discontinued Agfa color slide film — grain-free, punchy, with vivid deep blues
recipes.tip: Very forgiving in mixed light — the author never changed temperature between scenes; a diffusion filter softens highlights.

recipes.name: Zetra 100
recipes.film: Author original — amber-blue harmony, buttery soft contrast, true to life with character
recipes.tip: Color shifts go unnoticed in neutral scenes — it shines in vibrant, colorful landscapes.

recipes.name: Rose Spectra
recipes.film: Author original — creamy tones with a delicate rose cast that flatters skin
recipes.tip: Too subtle for lovers of loud color and deep contrast — pick it when you want warmth and quiet emotion.

recipes.name: Asteroid City (Kodak Vision 200T)
recipes.film: Kodak Vision3 200T cine film as graded in Wes Anderson's Asteroid City — warm pastel
recipes.tip: Overexpose V1 by +1 to +1.7 stops for pastel colors; V2 has more contrast and can be shot at 0 to +1.

recipes.name: Ayon 200
recipes.film: Author original — bold monochromatic yellow, sun-baked amber like Chungking Express
recipes.tip: Turns skies green and deepens reds — use it to make ordinary scenes feel sun-drenched and dramatic.

recipes.name: Kodak Portra 800
recipes.film: High-speed Portra — buttery soft contrast, famous for flattering natural skin tones
recipes.tip: Overexpose by one stop for the bright, vibrant Portra look; minimal editing needed beyond contrast.

recipes.name: Phoenix Harman 200
recipes.film: Harman/Ilford's first color film (2023) — grainy, ultra-contrasty, vibrant and warm
recipes.tip: Shadows and highlights clip hard and are tough to recover — skip it if you like re-grading colors in post.

recipes.name: Kodak Gold V1
recipes.film: Kodak's beloved consumer film — warm golden hues and easy, nostalgic charm
recipes.tip: The community's most beloved recipe and a great starter — it shines brightest in golden-hour light.

recipes.name: Kodak Gold V2
recipes.film: Twist on classic Gold — warmer, more vibrant, with a subtle teal shift in skies

recipes.name: Kodachrome V1
recipes.film: Iconic Kodachrome slide film — deep, fairly accurate colors with a slightly cool lean

recipes.name: Kodachrome V2
recipes.film: 1950s medium-format Kodachrome — golden skin tones and deep velvety blues
recipes.tip: Built on a complementary blue-amber harmony — a go-to when you want golden skin tones.

recipes.name: Kodachrome V3
recipes.film: Warm 1950s Kodachrome take — slightly desaturated, dusty cyan skies, teal-amber harmony

recipes.name: Estra 500
recipes.film: Author original — desaturated pastel look with a gentle pink cast, dreamy and soft
recipes.tip: Plays nicely with soft backlight or overcast skies — gentle highlights without harsh contrast; great for weddings.

recipes.name: Magic Spice
recipes.film: Author original — true-to-life colors with a hint of magic; born on an autumn hike
recipes.tip: Skin leans slightly desaturated toward red and skies go violet-blue — flattering portraits without editing.

recipes.name: Amarelo 30D
recipes.film: Author original — Super-8 'Show Me How' vibe: amber-yellow skin, rich teal skies
recipes.tip: A love-it-or-hate-it look — best in natural light; golden hour makes the sun-kissed skin glow even more.

recipes.name: Vektro 100
recipes.film: Author original — high contrast, slightly desaturated, blue-amber street look
recipes.tip: Made for bold, iconic images — try it for portraits or fashion too if you like an edgier look.

recipes.name: Oktar
recipes.film: Author original — crunchy tonality and cool, rich colors built for landscapes
recipes.tip: Don't box it into nature shots — the author encourages using it on your own terms.

recipes.name: Solara 100
recipes.film: Author original — sun-kissed golden warmth, faded shadows, dreamy summer glow
recipes.tip: Underexpose for moody magenta skies, overexpose for soft teal skies and pinkish skin tones.

recipes.name: Kodak Pro Image 100
recipes.film: Kodak's heat-resistant pro negative for tropical markets — golden skin, teal skies
recipes.tip: Shines in sunny, daylight-rich scenes; in mixed light expect to make small manual tweaks.

recipes.name: Orthochromatic
recipes.film: 1880s-style red-blind B&W — reds go dark, skies blow bright, punchy surreal contrast
recipes.tip: Overexpose by +0.5 to +1 stop or skin tones come out very dark — an experimental look, not for everyday portraits.

recipes.name: Senova Light
recipes.film: Author original — soft warm nostalgia; pinkish skin, greens shifting yellow-orange
recipes.tip: The author's first nostalgic portrait profile — a must-try for flattering beauty portraits.

recipes.name: Ektar 100
recipes.film: Kodak's ultra-vivid fine-grain negative — famously the most saturated print film
recipes.tip: One of the author's favorites and barely changed since release — versatile from portraits to landscapes.

recipes.name: Sony Nostalgic Neg
recipes.film: Author original — magenta-tinged look inspired by old, damaged vintage film photos
recipes.tip: Not an emulation of Fuji's Nostalgic Neg — the magenta cast gives shots a magical, aged feel.

recipes.name: Zero Mute
recipes.film: Author original — desaturated yet warm, faded hidden-gem look

recipes.name: Fortia 50
recipes.film: Rare limited-run Fuji slide film — ultra-vivid, deep colors with an otherworldly punch
recipes.tip: The author loves it for street; try it on portraits for a highly stylized, bold look.

recipes.name: Velvia Pro
recipes.film: Fuji Velvia slide film — legendary super-saturated landscape colors
recipes.tip: Punchy options like Velvia Pro thrive in bright, sunny conditions.

recipes.name: Provia RX
recipes.film: Built on real-world Provia scans — saturated colors, deep shadows, strong magenta cast
recipes.tip: Distinct from Fuji's Provia sim — orange skin against a deep blue cast; great beyond portraits too.

recipes.name: Sony Classic Negative
recipes.film: Author's take on the classic-negative look — balanced tones with a soft retro vibe
recipes.tip: It shines in vibrant, colorful scenes like nature — expect less impact in muted cityscapes.

recipes.name: Kodak Portra 400
recipes.film: The classic pro portrait negative — peachy skin tones with a slight magenta hue

recipes.name: Kodak Portra 160
recipes.film: Low-speed Portra — creamy colors, smooth contrast, classic soft Kodak vibe

recipes.name: Classic Chrome
recipes.film: Fuji's muted documentary-chrome look — a classic for Sony and Fuji shooters alike
recipes.tip: One of the safe, natural-looking recipes the author recommends starting with.

recipes.name: Cinestill 800T
recipes.film: Tungsten cine film beloved for neon-soaked night scenes and red halation
recipes.tip: Made for night-time use — one of the author's picks for low-light shooting.

recipes.name: Vespera
recipes.film: Author original — cool-toned nighttime look, cold yet enchanting

recipes.name: Veniliqum
recipes.film: Author original — warm-green night look for gritty city and crime-thriller moods
recipes.tip: Great under sodium-vapor street lights, which are incredibly hard to balance out.

recipes.name: Gold Luxe
recipes.film: Author original — bold warm night recipe that embraces sodium-vapor glow
recipes.tip: Instead of fighting warm night lights, this recipe leans into them for dramatic, high-impact shots.

recipes.name: Midred Infra
recipes.film: Inspired by infrared cine film (I Am Cuba, 1964) — skin and yellows pop, skies deepen

recipes.name: NeoMax
recipes.film: Author original — bright, dreamy B&W with light skin tones and a soft nostalgic glow

recipes.name: Ilford Delta 3200
recipes.film: Ilford's high-speed B&W — soft, balanced tones with high dynamic range
recipes.tip: The author recommends it for portraits and wedding photography.

recipes.name: Ilford HP5
recipes.film: Ilford's classic all-purpose B&W — well balanced with soft contrast

recipes.name: Kosmo Pan
recipes.film: B&W recipe with a muted, slightly surreal tonality for offbeat portraits

recipes.name: DreamNeg
recipes.film: Author original — warm, whimsical, super-soft tones; a warm hug for your JPEGs

recipes.name: Chronochrome
recipes.film: Author original — experimental recipe with bold, creative color shifts

recipes.name: AcidNom
recipes.film: Author original — psychedelic palette, deep unrealistic hues, a magic-forest trip
recipes.tip: Use it to see familiar surroundings in a completely new, fantastical way.

recipes.name: RedScale Ultra
recipes.film: Mimics a red lens filter / redscale film — rich crimson tones, dramatic contrast
recipes.tip: Perfect when you want a surreal, otherworldly atmosphere.

recipes.name: GAF 500
recipes.film: GAF/Anscochrome 500 — first ISO 500 film; grainy, amber-leaning, softly desaturated

recipes.name: Classic Cinema
recipes.film: Author original — sunny, bright 90s-movie look with subtle color shifts

recipes.name: Ektachrome
recipes.film: Kodak's cool-toned slide film — vibrant colors that shift with the light
recipes.tip: Its colors shift with lighting conditions — treat every shot as an experiment.

recipes.name: Kodak Ultra Max 400
recipes.film: Kodak's everyday consumer film — natural look with a slight, warm film touch
recipes.tip: A great starter recipe if you want a subtle film look without going overboard.

recipes.name: Kodak Color Plus 200
recipes.film: Kodak's budget classic — like a subtler Kodak Gold with more accurate skin tones
recipes.tip: Choose it over Kodak Gold when you want warmth but more accurate skin tones.

recipes.name: Sony Eterna
recipes.film: Eterna-style flat cine look tuned for a ready-to-go image straight out of camera

recipes.name: EvPro+
recipes.film: Author original — versatile all-rounder: dynamic range, rich color, strong low light
recipes.tip: The author's long-time go-to when 8-bit S-Log is too noisy — ready out of camera with room to grade.

recipes.name: Fuji 400H
recipes.film: Fujicolor Pro 400H — cool pastel wedding-film look; recipe adds richer color

recipes.name: Fuji Provia
recipes.film: Fuji's standard slide-film simulation — balanced, true-to-life colors
recipes.tip: Listed among the classic, natural recipes for beginners.

recipes.name: LeicaChrome
recipes.film: Modeled on Leica M9 JPEGs — classic CCD digital-chrome color rendering
